title: MultiAxisMachineElement Object
description: Machine element representing multi-axis machine settings.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: createFeedrateSettings
methods.description: Creates a MultiAxisFeedrateSettings specialized object from the given input.

methods.name: createFeedrateSettingsInput
methods.description: Creates a MultiAxisFeedrateSettingsInput object to be used as input for creating MultiAxisFeedrateSettings objects.

methods.name: createRetractAndReconfigureSettings
methods.description: Creates a MultiAxisRetractAndReconfigureSettings object. Set this object on the retractAndReconfigureSettings property to apply the changes.

methods.name: createSingularitySettings
methods.description: Creates a MultiAxisSingularitySettings object. Set this object on the singularitySettings property to apply the changes.

methods.name: deleteMe
methods.description: Delete this multi-axis machine element from the machine.

methods.name: staticTypeId
methods.description: Identifying name for all elements of this type. Pass this to the itemByType or itemById methods of MachineElements to filter to elements of this type.

properties.name: elementId
properties.description: Identifier for this element. Unique within an element type.

properties.name: feedrateSettings
properties.description: The multi-axis feedrate settings for this machine. For changes to to this object to take effect, re-assign them to this property. Cannot be set to null.

properties.name: isUsingVirtualTooltip
properties.description: Specifies if the position of the virtual tool tip (tool end) should be output. Only relevant for rotary head axes.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: retractAndReconfigureSettings
properties.description: The multi-axis retract and reconfigure settings for this machine. For changes to to this object to take effect, re-assign them to this property. To not use multi-axis retract and reconfigure, set this to null.

properties.name: singularitySettings
properties.description: The multi-axis kinematics settings for this machine. For changes to to this object to take effect, re-assign them to this property. To not use multi-axis kinematics, set this to null.

properties.name: typeId
properties.description: Identifier for this type of machine element.

classType.title: MultiAxisMachineElement.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

createFeedrateSettings.title: MultiAxisMachineElement.createFeedrateSettings Method
createFeedrateSettings.description: Creates a MultiAxisFeedrateSettings specialized object from the given input.
createFeedrateSettings.return_description: The specialized MultiAxisFeedrateSettings object created from the input.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: The input object containing the settings to create the MultiAxisFeedrateSettings object. Set this object on the feedrateSettings property to apply the changes.

createFeedrateSettingsInput.title: MultiAxisMachineElement.createFeedrateSettingsInput Method
createFeedrateSettingsInput.description: Creates a MultiAxisFeedrateSettingsInput object to be used as input for creating MultiAxisFeedrateSettings objects.

createRetractAndReconfigureSettings.title: MultiAxisMachineElement.createRetractAndReconfigureSettings Method
createRetractAndReconfigureSettings.description: Creates a MultiAxisRetractAndReconfigureSettings object. Set this object on the retractAndReconfigureSettings property to apply the changes.

createSingularitySettings.title: MultiAxisMachineElement.createSingularitySettings Method
createSingularitySettings.description: Creates a MultiAxisSingularitySettings object. Set this object on the singularitySettings property to apply the changes.

deleteMe.title: MultiAxisMachineElement.deleteMe Method
deleteMe.description: Delete this multi-axis machine element from the machine.

staticTypeId.title: MultiAxisMachineElement.staticTypeId Method
staticTypeId.description: Identifying name for all elements of this type. Pass this to the itemByType or itemById methods of MachineElements to filter to elements of this type.

elementId.title: MultiAxisMachineElement.elementId Property
elementId.description: Identifier for this element. Unique within an element type.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = multiAxisMachineElement_var.elementId

feedrateSettings.description: The multi-axis feedrate settings for this machine. For changes to to this object to take effect, re-assign them to this property. Cannot be set to null.

# Set the value of the property.
multiAxisMachineElement_var.feedrateSettings = propertyValue
